meaning: adj.膨胀的,夸张的,通货膨胀的
examples: They inflated their clients' medical injuries and treatment to defraud insurance companies.
He sang as he walked, and he walked as he sang, and got more inflated every minute.
He or she will instead point out, constructively and tactfully, exactly how their inflated sense of deservingness is somewhat distorted.
Something funny was going on with the books, suggesting that revenues and profits had been inflated.
During a credit expansion asset values become inflated, and credit-fueled spending tends to drive up corporate profitability.
